behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` returns the correct sum for A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks parser/range resolution/function dispatch/value store handoff, not just one function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are disqualified by behavior text (only in-memory formula evaluation, no OS/network/subprocess, no AI call).
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 values, set target cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run recalculation through normal engine pipeline, assert computed result.

behaviors.description: Cascaded reference evaluation works (`=B1+1` where `B1` is `=A1*2`).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in module interaction (dependency graph + evaluator + reference resolver). Higher levels are rejected because behavior text is purely in-process cell computation with no external infra and no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: In one workbook/sheet, set A1, B1 formula, and C1 formula referencing B1; recalc and assert C1 equals expected cascaded value.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1↔B1) returns circular reference error, not infinite loop.
behaviors.justification: This is graph-level behavior across multiple cells/modules; unit-only checks can miss cycle detection in wiring. System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=`=B1` and B1=`=A1` via normal workbook APIs, trigger evaluation, assert circular-reference error surfaced and evaluation terminates.

behaviors.description: `VLOOKUP` exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: Needs formula parser + function registry + table range lookup + error propagation together. Rejected higher levels because behavior text contains no external I/O and no AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Build lookup table cells in-memory, evaluate `VLOOKUP(...,FALSE)` for found and missing keys through full engine path, assert value and error contract.

behaviors.description: Nested `IF` conditions evaluate to correct branch results.
behaviors.justification: Nested expression behavior can fail at parser/evaluator boundary even if individual IF logic unit tests pass. System/Agentic/Workflow are rejected by behavior text (in-memory deterministic formula evaluation only).
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to values spanning branches, evaluate nested `IF` formula via standard engine evaluation flow, assert outputs `low`/`medium`/`high` appropriately.

behaviors.description: `CONCATENATE` joins with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is primarily single-function semantics (string join + empty handling) and does not require additional cross-module or infra reality signal beyond existing unit scope. Higher levels are disqualified by text: no external I/O, no model call, no orchestration chain.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test CONCATENATE evaluator/function object with representative inputs including empty cells and separators; assert exact output string.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves when sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Requires workbook/sheet namespace resolution plus evaluator handoff across modules. System is rejected because behavior text specifies no real OS/network/process boundary; Agentic/Workflow rejected (no AI dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1/Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1 and formula in Sheet1 referencing it, recalc through engine, assert resolved value.

behaviors.description: Changing a referenced cell triggers dependent recomputation in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-handoff across dependency tracking and scheduler modules; unit-only can miss ordering bugs. Higher levels rejected by behavior text: in-memory recalculation only, no external infra, no AI decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Create dependency chain/diamond, mutate upstream cell, run incremental recalc, assert final dependent values and (if observable) evaluation order/topological constraints.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and dependent computed values.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is cross-module state handoff (edit command/history + calc graph + value cache). System rejected because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess or external boundary readback; Agentic/Workflow not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Edit formula through normal API, verify changed dependents, execute undo, assert formula text and all impacted dependent values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save workbook to disk and reload with identical formulas/values and floating-point precision.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + ROUND-TRIP side effect: behavior explicitly requires real disk persistence and readback correctness across serialization boundary. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due concrete text: binary file I/O only, no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real temporary file path on the OS filesystem, save workbook with representative formulas/floats, reopen via loader, assert formulas and numeric values (with precision expectations) are unchanged.
